title: DomoDreams Panel config (panels JSON)
description: The single config document consumed by BOTH sides (PLAN.md §3.3 + §3.6). The app renders pages/tiles and ignores `bindings`; the HA integration executes `bindings` and ignores visual props. Kept intentionally permissive on visual-only fields (the app tolerates unknowns) but strict on structure the integration relies on.

theme.description: Panel theme name (generated by tools/theme-import). Kept in sync with the kit THEMES registry + the app isThemeName() validator.

alarmPage.description: Alarm (Alarmo) keypad page config (PLAN.md §3.3, Phase 5). The panel talks to Alarmo DIRECTLY over its native MQTT topics (https://github.com/nielsfaber/alarmo#mqtt) — NOT via this integration. All fields optional; the app fills the master-alarm topic defaults. The PIN is entered on-device and published ONLY to the non-retained command topic — never stored here or on any retained topic.

commandTopic.description: Alarmo command topic — the SINGLE master topic (default 'alarmo/command'). The panel publishes JSON {command:'arm_home'|'arm_away'|'disarm', code, area?}; the area is selected via the payload, NOT a per-area topic.

eventTopic.description: Alarmo event topic (JSON feedback: INVALID_CODE_PROVIDED, FAILED_TO_ARM, ...). Per-area = 'alarmo/<area>/event'; master default 'alarmo/event'.

area.description: Alarmo area slug to target (sent as `area` in the command payload, e.g. 'panel'). Omit for the master alarm.

actions.description: Which action buttons to show (default = all three). Always rendered; disabled contextually by the app.

codeArmRequired.description: Whether ARMING needs a PIN (gates the arm buttons; disarm always needs one). Default true. If Alarmo rejects with NO_CODE_PROVIDED the panel prompts anyway.

screensaverWhenArmed.description: When armed, show this alarm page as the screensaver instead of the clock. Default true; false opts out.

cameraPage.description: Camera page: a live MJPEG view (480x270, 16:9) above a control strip with a PTZ D-pad and a camera selector. The INTEGRATION mirrors each camera entity onto its retained state/{key} topic with an absolute `stream` URL carrying HA's rotating camera access token (from entity_picture); the app renders that URL in a WebView . Only the selected camera on the currently-visible page ever streams. PTZ entity ids are EXPLICIT (never derived from the camera's name) so the page works with any brand.

cameras.description: Cameras selectable on this page, in order. The first is shown on arrival. Prefer a camera's SD/substream entity: the video area is 480px wide, so an HD stream is wasted decode on the panel.

entity.description: The HA camera entity to stream, e.g. 'camera.cam_cucina_esterna_sd_stream'.

name.description: Label in the camera selector. Defaults to a prettified entity id.

ptz.description: Pan/tilt targets. Only the directions present here get an enabled arrow; the rest render disabled. Ids are explicit because no naming convention generalises across brands (Tapo, Reolink, ONVIF all differ).

baseUrl.description: Override the HA base URL the stream URL is built from (the integration otherwise uses helpers.network.get_url). Escape hatch for a multi-homed/containerised HA whose auto-detected URL the panel cannot reach. Integration-only: never sent to the app.

keepOnPage.description: Suppress the stage-1 inactivity return-to-default-page while this page is showing, so you are not yanked away mid-watch. Default true. Bounded by the stage-2 screensaver, which still fires and stops the stream.

keepAwake.description: Also hold off the stage-2 screensaver while streaming. Default FALSE, deliberately: an unattended live stream on an always-on wall panel (backlight burn-in, 1.4 Mbit/s, constant JPEG decode) is exactly what the screensaver exists to prevent. The screensaver wakes on proximity, so walking up brings the camera back in about a second.

holdRepeatMs.description: PTZ press-and-hold repeat interval in ms (0 = no repeat, single press only). Default 400.

ptzTarget.description: One PTZ direction. Either a shorthand entity_id string -- the bridge derives the service from its domain (button -> button.press, switch -> switch.turn_on, script -> script.turn_on, scene -> scene.turn_on) -- or a full binding action / ordered action list, which is executed verbatim by the same executor tile bindings use. The object form is the escape hatch for cameras whose PTZ is a service call rather than an entity, e.g. {"action":"call","service":"onvif.ptz","entity_id":"camera.front","data":{"pan":"LEFT"}}.

iconLow.description: dimmer only — icon at the low/dim end; the bright/high end uses `icon`.

statusDot.description: false/absent = no dot; true = static dot (as before); object = dynamic dot bound to an entity (PLAN.md §3.6): the integration mirrors the entity with a generic `value` (raw HA state) and the app compares it on-device. Defaults: operator=equals, compareTo='on', color=theme status red.

optimistic.description: Optimistic UI (button/state/switcher/split): show the tapped state instantly and reconcile from the mirror. Default true; false = reflect confirmed device state only.

items.description: switcher only — the segmented options (hero scene row). 2-6 items, unique values; direct-select: tapping a segment publishes {action:'state', value}. Per-value bindings use the 'state:<value>' key (PLAN.md §3.6). Active segment follows mirrored state/{key}.value.

buttons.description: split only — the 2-4 sub-buttons carved into one shared raised surface (PLAN.md §3.6). Each segment toggles independently; event button id = '<tileId>.<segId>'. Segment visual state (off|on|inset) is evaluated app-side on HA mirrors; transition bindings live PER SEGMENT keyed press:off|on|inset.

dimmerSurface.description: dimmer only — track body style. Default 'inset' (recessed channel); 'raised' renders the outset variant that sits proud of the surface.

labelPosition.description: Where the label sits relative to the glyph: 'below' (default) = ICON over LABEL; 'above' = LABEL over ICON. On a split, each SEGMENT carries its own (the tile-level one does not apply to split segments).

rockers.description: rocker only — the 1-2 on/off switches stacked vertically in this tile. Each is on/off BY DEFINITION (a light/fan/switch): it shows the entity's state and a tap toggles it. No configurable actions.

entity.description: The on/off entity this switch controls.

icon.description: Glyph in the switch's right half.

bindings.description: Keys are event names (press|double|long|release|dim|toggle) or per-value keys for state buttons (state:<value>). Each value is a single action OR an array of actions run in order (multi-action, global upgrade PLAN.md §3.6).

splitSegment.description: One sub-button of a split tile (PLAN.md §3.6). `id` is unique within the tile; the event button id is '<tileId>.<segId>'.

labelPosition.description: Where the label sits relative to the glyph: 'below' (default) = ICON over LABEL; 'above' = LABEL over ICON. On a split, each SEGMENT carries its own (the tile-level one does not apply to split segments).

splitSegmentState.description: HA-driven visual state of a split segment (PLAN.md §3.6): evaluated app-side on mirrors, precedence inset > on > off, fail-safe off. `entity` is the default source for both rules; each rule may override it. The integration mirrors every referenced entity with a generic `value`.

splitSegmentRule.description: A split-segment state rule — same operator grammar as a status dot. Defaults: operator=equals, compareTo='on'.

splitSegmentBindings.description: Split-segment TRANSITION bindings keyed by the segment's state AT PRESS time (PLAN.md §3.6). The panel publishes {button:'<tileId>.<segId>', action:'press', state:'off|on|inset'}; the bridge routes the matching press:<state>. Each value is a single action OR an array of actions run in order.

bindingList.description: A single binding action OR an ordered array of actions (multi-action upgrade, PLAN.md §3.6). Arrays are executed in order by the bridge; min 1 entry.
